pr.title: Add numerous JObj symbols and HSD_EraseRect
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly symbolication pass in asm/disasm.s that replaced many anonymous func_ labels and call targets with known HSD JObj names, plus named func_80374AA0 as HSD_EraseRect. The PR body says the JObj symbols came from past work and that HSD_EraseRect is used for screen clearing. Evidence indicates this was a naming-only assembly update: one file changed, with equal added and deleted line counts, and the diff shows labels and branch-call target names changing while instruction addresses/opcodes remain the same.

key_files.role: Only changed file; updated HSD/JObj function labels and call-site symbol names throughout the raw disassembly.

what_changed: Renamed func_80007D68 to HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr and updated many call sites to use HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr.
Renamed func_80374AA0 to HSD_EraseRect and updated at least one screen-clearing call site to call HSD_EraseRect.

Introduced many JObj global labels in the HSD range, including HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, HSD_JObjMakeMatrix, HSD_JObjReqAnimAll, HSD_JObjAddAnim, HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnim, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_JObjSetDefaultClass, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_JObjResolveRefs, HSD_JObjResolveRefsAll, HSD_JObjUnref, HSD_JObjUnrefThis, HSD_JObjRemove, HSD_JObjRemoveAll, HSD_JObjAddChild, HSD_JObjReparent, HSD_JObjAddNext, HSD_JObjGetPrev, HSD_JObjGetFlags, HSD_JObjSetFlags, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, HSD_JObjClearFlags, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, HSD_JObjAlloc, HSD_JObjSetCurrent, HSD_JObjGetCurrent, HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub, and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub.

Updated downstream call sites across early-game and HSD-related assembly to use the newly named JObj helpers instead of func_80371D00, func_80371CE8, func_80371F00, func_80373078, func_803732E8, func_8036FB5C, and other anonymous targets.
No C source, headers, structs, or build logic are evidenced in the slice; the change is confined to disassembly labels.
smart_moves: Applied symbol names both at function definitions and at all visible branch-call users, making the assembly more searchable and reducing future duplicate identification work.

Grouped many related HSD_JObj names in one pass, which helps establish a coherent API vocabulary for later decompilation of JObj animation, hierarchy, flags, matrix, allocation, and reference-management code.
Preserved addresses and instruction comments while changing labels, so the disassembly remains easy to verify against original code locations.
Named HSD_EraseRect with a concrete behavioral note from the PR body: it is used for screen clearing.
decomp_lessons: Early symbolication PRs can be high leverage even without C conversion: replacing func_ names with stable library-style names improves call graph readability for later matching work.
When a known library function is identified, update every call site in the assembly slice, not just the global label, so grep/search results reflect the new semantic name.

Equal added/deleted line counts in a disassembly-only PR are consistent with a rename pass; reviewers and future agents should still verify that addresses/opcodes are unchanged before treating it as non-behavioral.
For HAL/HSD code, preserve the HSD_ prefix and JObj subsystem prefix to keep symbols aligned with the underlying library naming scheme.
naming_conventions: HSD subsystem functions use the HSD_ prefix.
Joint object functions use HSD_JObj as the subsystem prefix.
Function names use CamelCase after the prefix, e.g. HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr, HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub.
Boolean/query-like JObj names can use forms such as HSD_JObjMtxIsDirty.
Recursive/all-node variants use the All suffix, e.g. HSD_JObjAddAnimAll, HSD_JObjAnimAll, HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll, HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll, HSD_JObjRemoveAll, HSD_JObjResolveRefsAll.
Internal helper variants use the Sub suffix, e.g. HSD_JObjSetupMatrixSub and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub.
Matrix abbreviation appears as Mtx in symbol names, as in HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr and HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Rename anonymous .global labels directly in asm/disasm.s while retaining the original address comments.
Update branch-call mnemonics from anonymous func_ targets to named HSD symbols at each call site.
Use repeated call-site patterns to validate symbol identity; HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr appears at many sites where code then works with JObj matrix data around offset 0x44.
Use known HSD API names from past work to bootstrap a more readable call graph before full C decompilation.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move these HSD_JObj symbols from asm/disasm.s into C source or headers.
Search for HSD_EraseRect in later commits to identify its prototype and graphics-state behavior.
Search for HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr call sites that access JObj offset 0x44 to support struct field naming.
Look for review or later corrections to these symbol names before relying on them for header declarations.
